---
title: "Setup"
subtitle: "Local and HPC environment prerequisites"
date: last-modified
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

This page covers everything required before running the pipeline or its test
suite — local development on macOS and HPC execution on the Rockfish cluster.

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'themeVariables': {'fontSize': '16px'}}}%%
flowchart TD
    classDef step fill:#4e9af1,stroke:#2c6fad,color:#fff
    classDef hpc fill:#9b59b6,stroke:#6c3483,color:#fff
    classDef data fill:#f5f5f5,stroke:#999,color:#333

    CLONE([Clone repository]):::data
    CHOICE{Target environment}:::data

    NF[Install Nextflow 24.10.x]:::step
    DOCKER[Install Docker Desktop]:::step
    R[Install R packages]:::step
    PY[Install Python + pytest]:::step
    BCFT[Install bcftools + tabix]:::step
    VCF[Generate test VCF]:::step
    LOCAL_DONE([Ready: local runs and tests]):::data

    ENV[conda activate nf24_env]:::hpc
    SING[Set NXF_SINGULARITY_CACHEDIR]:::hpc
    VCF_HPC[Generate test VCF on Rockfish]:::hpc
    HPC_DONE([Ready: HPC runs]):::data

    CLONE --> CHOICE
    CHOICE -- Local --> NF --> DOCKER --> R --> PY --> BCFT --> VCF --> LOCAL_DONE
    CHOICE -- Rockfish --> ENV --> SING --> VCF_HPC --> HPC_DONE
```


## Prerequisites

| Tool | Version | Required for |
|------|---------|-------------|
| Nextflow | `>=24.10.0,<25.0.0` | All pipeline runs |
| Docker Desktop | any | Local containerized runs, integration test collection |
| R | >= 4.1 | Unit and integration tests |
| Python | >= 3.9 | pytest tests for `bin/create_causal_vars.py` |
| bcftools | >= 1.16 | Test VCF generation (one-time) |
| tabix | any (htslib) | Test VCF generation (one-time) |
| Quarto | any | Rendering or publishing the documentation site |


## Local Setup

### Nextflow

The pipeline requires Nextflow `>=24.10.0,<25.0.0`. The `nextflow.preview.output`
publish block syntax used in `main.nf` is a preview feature in 24.x that is absent
in 25.x — installing the latest Nextflow will cause the pipeline to fail.

Install a pinned version using the official install script:

```bash
export NXF_VER=24.10.5
curl -s https://get.nextflow.io | bash
chmod +x nextflow
mv nextflow ~/.local/bin/   # move to any directory on PATH
```

Or install via conda:

```bash
conda install -c bioconda -c conda-forge nextflow=24.10.5
```

Verify the installed version:

```bash
nextflow -version
# Expected: version 24.10.x build ...
```

### Docker

Docker is required for containerized local runs (`-profile docker`) and for the
integration test collection script (`tests/collect_test_data.sh`). Stub runs do
not require Docker.

Install [Docker Desktop](https://www.docker.com/products/docker-desktop/) and
verify it is running:

```bash
docker run --rm hello-world
```

### R Packages

Unit and integration tests run with any R installation that has the required
packages. To reproduce the exact versions from the `andersenlab/r_packages:20250519`
container, restore from `renv.lock`:

```r
install.packages("renv")
renv::restore(lockfile = "renv.lock")
```

The lockfile covers packages used by `R_.*` Nextflow processes. `DB_MIGRATION_.*`
processes use a separate container (`andersenlab/migrate-sims:20260202`). Install
the DB migration packages manually if they are not already present:

```r
install.packages(c("arrow", "duckdb", "dplyr", "tidyr", "purrr",
                    "digest", "testthat", "readr", "data.table"))
```

See [Output Validation](testing-output-validation.qmd#r-package-lockfile) for
full details on the lockfile and how to regenerate it.

### Python

`tests/pytest/` contains tests for `bin/create_causal_vars.py`. The tests require
`numpy`, `pandas`, and `pytest`:

```bash
pip install "numpy==2.2.3" "pandas==2.2.3" pytest
```

To run tests inside the pipeline container instead (no local Python install required):

```bash
docker run --rm -v "$PWD":/work -w /work andersenlab/numpy:20250506 \
  pytest tests/pytest/
```

### bcftools and tabix

Required only for generating test VCF files locally. Not needed for stub runs or
unit tests. bcftools >= 1.16 is required.

**macOS (Homebrew):**

```bash
brew install htslib    # includes tabix
brew install bcftools
```

Verify:

```bash
bcftools --version | head -1   # should report 1.16 or later
tabix --version | head -1
```

### Quarto

Required only for rendering or publishing the documentation site. Not needed for
pipeline runs or tests.

[Install Quarto](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/) from the official site, then verify:

```bash
quarto --version
```

### Test VCF Generation

The test VCF (`data/test/test.vcf.gz`) is not committed to the repository due to
its size. This is a one-time step required before any non-stub local pipeline run.
Requires bcftools >= 1.16 and tabix.

The generate script accepts a local file path or an HTTPS URL from the CaeNDR S3
bucket. Using a URL is recommended — bcftools fetches only chromosomes I, II, and V
via tabix random access, avoiding a multi-GB download:

```bash
# Using an HTTPS URL (recommended — fetches only needed chromosomes)
bash data/test/generate_test_vcf.sh "https://..."

# Or using a local copy of the source VCF
bash data/test/generate_test_vcf.sh /path/to/WI.20220216.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz
```

See [Local End-to-End Testing](testing-local-e2e.qmd#generating-test-data) for the
full generate commands, including multi-species VCFs for the `test_three_species`
profile.


## HPC Setup (Rockfish)

### Clone

```bash
git clone https://github.com/AndersenLab/nemascan-sims-nf
cd nemascan-sims-nf
```

### Conda Environment

The `nf24_env` conda environment on Rockfish provides Nextflow 24.10.x and its
Java runtime. Source your bash profile first so that `conda` is on PATH:

```bash
source ~/.bash_profile
conda activate /data/eande106/software/conda_envs/nf24_env
```

Verify:

```bash
nextflow -version   # should report 24.10.x
```

### Singularity Cache

Container images are cached at `/vast/eande106/singularity`. Set
`NXF_SINGULARITY_CACHEDIR` so Nextflow finds them automatically without
re-pulling:

```bash
export NXF_SINGULARITY_CACHEDIR=/vast/eande106/singularity
```

Add this to `~/.bash_profile` to make it persistent across sessions:

```bash
echo 'export NXF_SINGULARITY_CACHEDIR=/vast/eande106/singularity' >> ~/.bash_profile
```

### SLURM Account and Custom Paths

`conf/rockfish.config` ships with defaults for the `eande106` account. Override
any of these on the command line without editing the config file:

| Parameter | Default | Description |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| `--slurm_account` | `eande106` | SLURM account name passed to `-A` |
| `--scratch_dir` | `/scratch4/eande106` | Nextflow work directory on scratch |
| `--baseDir` | `/vast/eande106` | Base directory for data and Singularity cache |
| `--dataDir` | `/vast/eande106/data` | Data directory |
| `--softwareDir` | `/data/eande106/software` | Software directory |

Example — run under a different account:

```bash
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test,rockfish \
  --slurm_account myaccount \
  --scratch_dir /scratch4/myaccount \
  --baseDir /vast/myaccount
```

### Test VCF Generation on Rockfish

Generate the test VCF from an interactive node. Request one via `interact`
(adjust partition as needed):

```bash
interact -n 1 -c 1 -a eande106 -m 64G -t 30
```

Load bcftools and **export PATH** before running the generate script. The
`module load` command modifies the current shell's PATH but does not export it;
without `export PATH`, the bash subprocess spawned by the script exits with
"bcftools not found":

```bash
module spider bcftools        # list available versions
module load bcftools/1.17     # any version >= 1.16
export PATH                   # required — exports module changes to subprocesses
```

Then generate the test VCF:

```bash
FULL_VCF=/vast/eande106/data/c_elegans/WI/variation/20210901/vcf/WI.20210901.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz
bash data/test/generate_test_vcf.sh "$FULL_VCF"
```

`data/test/test.20210901.vcf.gz` is a committed symlink that points to
`data/test/test.vcf.gz`. The script creates the symlink target on a fresh clone,
making the symlink valid for the first time.

See [HPC End-to-End Testing](testing-hpc.qmd#generating-the-test-vcf-on-rockfish)
for additional context on this step.


::: {.callout-tip}
## Next steps
With the environment in place, verify pipeline wiring with a stub run:
[Local End-to-End Testing](testing-local-e2e.qmd)
:::
